methodsAttempted.query: site:uthm.edu.my UTHM artificial intelligence policy academic integrity generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned an official UTHM Legal Advisor Office plagiarism regulation PDF, UTHM publisher research PDFs, an official news opinion page, and profile pages. Only the Legal Advisor Office regulation was policy-grade for academic integrity claims.
Firecrawl search returned UTHM's Digital Teaching and Learning Policy PDF on maya.uthm.edu.my, plus the plagiarism regulation and profile/research pages.
robots.txt disallows Joomla administrative and system paths but not the public regulation URL or its /file redirect. A HEAD request followed the official page to an inline PDF with HTTP 200.
robots.txt disallows administrative and system paths, including /api/, but not /images/sumber/. A HEAD request to the policy PDF returned HTTP 200.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found two policy-grade UTHM PDFs: the Senate-endorsed Plagiarism Prevention Regulations and the Senate-approved 2025 Digital Teaching and Learning Policy. Research articles, news/opinion material, and profile pages were rejected for policy claims.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Peraturan Pencegahan Plagiarisme Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia
artifacts.queryUsed: site:uthm.edu.my UTHM artificial intelligence policy academic integrity generative AI
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official UTHM Legal Advisor Office PDF, Senate endorsement No. 9 session 2023/2024 times 238 dated 8 July 2024; includes Malay and English text and expressly mentions work generated by generative AI in the plagiarism definition.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Dasar Pengajaran dan Pembelajaran Digital Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official UTHM Virtual Learning Centre PDF, approved by Academic Studies Committee on 17 December 2024 and University Senate on 10 February 2025; section 6 addresses LMS, teaching aids, and generative AI.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: Potential Benefits and Challenges of Generative Artificial ...
artifacts.notes: Official UTHM publisher host, but the result is a periodical article about generative AI benefits and challenges, not an institutional policy or guidance source.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: With the rise of generative artificial intelligence (AI), anyone can create convincing deepfakes or falsified narratives with little effort.
artifacts.notes: Official UTHM News page, but it is a general digital-ethics article and does not establish UTHM policy requirements.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: ACADEMIC QUALIFICATION. DOKTOR FALSAFAH ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE, UNIVERSITI PUTRA MALAYSIA (2008)
artifacts.notes: Official profile page mentioning artificial intelligence expertise, but it is not a UTHM policy or guidance source.

targets.sourceTitle: Peraturan Pencegahan Plagiarisme Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia
Dasar Pengajaran dan Pembelajaran Digital Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia

stopConditions: Stop after official UTHM policy PDFs provide direct evidence for AI-related academic integrity and teaching/assessment claims.
Do not infer university-wide policy from UTHM publisher articles, news articles, community profiles, or research pages.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Peraturan Pencegahan Plagiarisme Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia
Dasar Pengajaran dan Pembelajaran Digital Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia

artifacts.claimText: UTHM's plagiarism prevention regulations apply to students, former students, staff, former staff, and relevant current or former university community members, and define plagiarism to include submitting or publishing all or part of work generated by generative artificial intelligence as one's own work.

citation.citationTitle: Peraturan Pencegahan Plagiarisme Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UTHM's Digital Teaching and Learning Policy permits the use of generative artificial intelligence in teaching and learning processes, including learning content, activity implementation, and assessment, subject to suitability, assessment effectiveness, compliance with applicable guidelines, laws and regulations, and good principles, values, and ethics in the course field.

citation.citationTitle: Dasar Pengajaran dan Pembelajaran Digital Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: UTHM's Digital Teaching and Learning Policy says assessment should be designed to be contextual, fair, transparent, and personalized while taking into account the risk of generative AI misuse in written assignments, and that continuous assessment involving presentations, question-and-answer, and viva voce should be prioritized to assess students' actual mastery.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow academic-integrity claim because the official regulation directly states scope and includes generative AI-generated work presented as one's own in the plagiarism definition.
Approved as a teaching-and-learning policy claim because section 6.3 directly permits KBG use in PdP while attaching conditions; it is not framed as unrestricted tool approval.
Approved as an assessment-design claim; the wording preserves the source's should/priority language and does not convert it into a prohibition.

artifacts.title: UTHM AI Policy Evidence Review
artifacts.summary: Official UTHM evidence supports three agent-reviewed candidate claims: the plagiarism regulations include AI-generated work submitted as one's own within plagiarism, the 2025 digital teaching and learning policy permits generative AI use in teaching and learning under stated conditions, and that same policy directs assessment design to account for generative AI misuse risk in written assignments.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The crawl found no standalone UTHM generative AI student policy beyond the Digital Teaching and Learning Policy and the Plagiarism Prevention Regulations.
Claims are scoped to the cited official documents and are agent_reviewed only.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
